title: Something in the Air
summary: Onboard the reader through Ada's lived-in pre-shift routine, then introduce subtle air wrongness and a small observatory contradiction that turns mood into method.

narrative.newInfo: Two low-amplitude anomalies appear in separate domains (drive-time perception and observatory behavior), implying possible pattern rather than isolated glitch.
narrative.dripHook: If the air anomaly and observatory contradiction rhyme, what rule is starting to emerge?
narrative.payoffDelivered: Reader onboarding is complete: Ada's world is inhabitable, Ada is trustworthy, and the mystery is procedural rather than purely spooky.
narrative.notes: Scene plan (leaning): 1) false-morning wake + warm lived-in routine, 2) drive-time subtle anomaly with self-testing, 3) observatory startup grounding, 4) micro contradiction in system world, 5) private commitment to monitor/log before reporting.

rewardTokens.description: Warm, lived-in pre-shift ritual gives low-friction entry and makes Ada's interiority pleasant to inhabit.
Ada actively tests possible mundane explanations rather than surrendering to vibes.
Chapter closes on private monitoring commitment, creating clean propulsion into the rupture chapter.

modelUpdate.before: Unusual perception can likely be dismissed as fatigue/environment noise.
modelUpdate.shift: A second small contradiction appears in a different domain.
modelUpdate.after: Treat as possible structured anomaly and gather disciplined observations.
